source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane - East Breaks 602 A

actions_taken: Injured party reported to facility Medic for evaluation and first aid
Injured party flown via field helicopter to ERA base in Lake Charles, LA
Injured party transported to All Industrial Medical Services in Houma, LA for treatment
Injured party reassigned to light duty pending full release
event.summary: On January 10, 2015 at approximately 1100 hours, a contract construction worker sustained a finger crush injury while installing an 8" ANSI 900 isolation valve on the High Pressure separator #1 at Anadarko Petroleum's East Breaks 602 A Nansen SPAR platform. The worker's left index finger was smashed between a hammer handle and hammer wrench when the 2# hammer deflected off the HP separator vessel support in a confined work area. The injured party was diagnosed with multiple fractures and dislocation of the second bone of the left index finger and underwent surgery on January 14, 2015.
key_phrases: less than one year offshore experience

hazards.name: Confined work space with restricted access
Crush hazard between tool and equipment

threats.name: Inadequate work area planning for confined space bolting operations
Worker failure to recognize confined area hazards

consequences.name: Finger crush injury with fractures and dislocation
Lost work time and medical treatment requirement
Permanent disability or reduced hand function

controls.name: Work area design and layout planning

supporting_text: The isolation valve bolts were in a confined area with little room to strike the hammer wrench with the 2# hammer.

The isolation valve flange was in close proximity (confined area) to the HP separator vessel support. This restricted the area that the Injured Party (IP) had to strike the Cameron wrench/hammer wrench to tighten the flange bolts on the isolation valve flange.

controls.name: Hazard recognition and awareness training
controls.barrier_role: Prevent worker failure to recognize confined area hazards

supporting_text: The IP failed to recognize the hazards of working in a confined area.
The IP had less than one year offshore experience.

controls.name: Tool control and securing procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hammer deflection and loss of control

supporting_text: One construction worker (CW) held the hammer wrench with a rope to secure the hammer wrench in place. The IP attempted to strike the hammer wrench with the 2# hammer. The 2# hammer deflected off the HP separator vessel support causing the IP's left index finger to be smashed between the hammer handle and the hammer wrench.

controls.name: Medical response and evacuation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity and provide timely medical treatment

supporting_text: The IP immediately reported to the facility Medic for evaluation and first aid.
The Medic determined that the IP's left index finger was broken and should be flown in for X-Rays and additional medical evaluation.
The IP was flown via the field helicopter to the ERA base in Lake Charles, LA then transported to All Industrial Medical Services in Houma, LA for treatment.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
